trm:term.trm:name: Semantic Search
trm:term.trm:definition: A retrieval mode that returns spec nodes ranked by meaning, not by identifier. The query is a natural-language string (or partial phrase); the ranker converts it to an embedding, compares against stored per-node embeddings, and returns the closest matches. Complements the XPath query command, which requires knowing the target node's id or namespace.

trm:term.trm:name: Structural Fingerprint
trm:term.trm:definition: A fixed-size bit vector encoding a node's structural position in the spec: its layer, namespace, ancestor-path n-grams, element-name hash, and incoming/outgoing relation types. Enables similarity comparisons based on structure alone, independent of textual content.

trm:term.trm:name: Concatenated Fingerprint
trm:term.trm:definition: The stored vector for one spec node: a dense text embedding concatenated with the node's structural fingerprint, resulting in a single fixed-length vector. A custom distance metric treats the two halves differently (cosine on text, Tanimoto on bits), so one index supports both semantic and structural retrieval simultaneously.

trm:term.trm:name: Sidecar Index
trm:term.trm:definition: The derived-artifact directory `.clayers/search/` colocated with the spec. Holds the binary vector index (`index.usearch`), the metadata store (`meta.sqlite`), and the build configuration (`config.json`). Gitignored via clayers adopt; never enters source control.

trm:term.trm:name: Incremental Rebuild
trm:term.trm:definition: A rebuild mode that re-embeds only nodes whose C14N content hash changed since the last index build. Reuses the node-hash computation path already maintained by the artifact drift detector, so the search cache and drift detection agree on what "changed" means.

trm:term.trm:name: Custom Metric
trm:term.trm:definition: A user-supplied distance function registered with the vector index at runtime. Clayers search registers a Rust closure via `usearch::Index::change_metric` that computes `alpha * cosine(text_half) + beta * tanimoto(struct_half)` on raw pointer slices. Invoked during HNSW graph traversal, not merely after the fact.

trm:term.trm:name: Context-Prepended Chunk
trm:term.trm:definition: The text fed to the embedder for a single spec node: a structural header `[layer=X path=A>B>C]` followed by the node's own text content. The header cheaply injects structural locality into the semantic embedding, so identical phrases in different layers end up in different neighbourhoods.

trm:term.trm:name: Tanimoto Similarity
trm:term.trm:definition: A similarity measure on binary vectors defined as the ratio of intersecting bits to union bits. Widely used in chemical-fingerprint retrieval. Applied here to the 256-bit structural fingerprint so two nodes sharing a layer, ancestor path, and relation signature score as similar regardless of prose differences.

pr:section.pr:title: Semantic Search Overview
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: What the clayers search command does, how it differs from query, and when to prefer each.
pr:section.pr:p: The clayers query command answers precise questions with XPath: "give me the definition of `term-drift`", "list all artifact mappings for `crates/clayers-spec/src/drift.rs`". It requires knowing identifiers and namespaces up front.

The clayers search command answers fuzzy questions with natural language: "which terms define drift?", "which plan items touch hashing?" It ranks nodes by semantic closeness to the query and reveals ids the caller did not know.
The two modes compose. A user can start with search "drift detection" to discover relevant node ids, then feed those ids into query expressions for precise extraction.
pr:section.pr:note: Structure matters in clayers specs, so search does not reduce everything to text. A 256-bit structural fingerprint rides alongside each text embedding, letting users rank by meaning, structure, or a weighted mix (`--alpha`, `--beta`).

pr:section.pr:title: Pipeline
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Chunker, embedder, fingerprint, index, metric, CLI — the pieces and how they fit.
pr:section.pr:p: Indexing walks the spec twice: first per-source-file to collect byte spans (xot's parse_with_span_info), then across the combined document to collect ancestor chains, layers, and relation incidence. The two passes merge into a set of context-prepended chunks, one per @id-bearing element.

Each chunk runs through two parallel pipelines. The text half is embedded with fastembed-rs (BGE-small-en-v1.5 by default, 384-dim). The structural half is compressed into the structural fingerprint (256 bits). Both halves are written to .clayers/search/index.usearch as a single concatenated 640-dim f32 vector.

Per-node metadata (id, file, line range, layer, namespace, content hash, preview, usearch key) lives in a sibling SQLite database .clayers/search/meta.sqlite. This doubles as the incremental-rebuild cache: rebuilds skip any node whose current content hash matches its stored one.

Querying embeds the user's string (without a context header, since queries are not structural). The embedder pads the structural half with zeros. usearch evaluates the custom metric (alpha * cosine(text) + beta * tanimoto(struct)) during HNSW traversal and returns top-k keys. The CLI joins those keys with meta.sqlite to produce a ranked table.

pr:section.pr:title: Two-Pass Chunking
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Why the chunker reads each file twice: to pair source-accurate line numbers with combined-document context.
pr:section.pr:p: xot's span information returns byte offsets, not line numbers, and is only available when a file is parsed with parse_with_span_info. The existing combined- document assembler parses without spans, so it cannot tell a caller where node X came from.

The chunker compensates with a pre-assembly pass. It reads each XML file in the spec, parses it with span info, and records (id, path, byte_start, byte_end) per @id-bearing element. A trivial byte_to_line helper (counting \n in the source string) converts byte offsets to 1-indexed line numbers.
Ancestor chains and relation incidence come from the assembled document. The chunker walks the combined tree and joins on @id against the span map to produce the final chunk record.

pr:section.pr:title: Structural Fingerprint Layout
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How 256 bits encode layer, namespace, ancestor path, element name, and relation types.
pr:section.pr:p: The fingerprint is a fixed 256-bit vector with six active segments and five reserved bits:

pr:property.pr:propdesc: Layer one-hot over the eleven clayers layers: prose, terminology, organization, relation, decision, source, plan, artifact, llm, revision, and index.
Ancestor-path 3-gram shingles. The ancestor chain's element localnames are joined with /; each 3-gram is hashed into [0, 128) and the corresponding bit is set.
Reserved for future growth and zero-filled.

pr:section.pr:p: The layout is frozen: a change in segment boundaries invalidates every stored fingerprint. Bumping config.json's fingerprint-version forces a full rebuild.

pr:section.pr:title: Sidecar Index Layout
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: What lives inside `.clayers/search/` and how the three files cooperate.
pr:section.pr:p: The sidecar holds three files:

pr:dlentry.pr:dd: A memory-mappable usearch index of 640-dim f32 vectors, one per @id-bearing node. The per-node concatenated fingerprint stores the text embedding in the first 384 dims and the structural fingerprint (bits cast to 0.0/1.0 f32s) in the last 256 dims.

A SQLite database with table nodes(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, file TEXT, line_start INTEGER, line_end INTEGER, layer TEXT, namespace TEXT, node_hash TEXT, preview TEXT, key INTEGER). Supplies human-readable fields for query output and serves as the incremental-rebuild cache.
Build configuration: model name, text-dim, struct-dim, build timestamp, spec revision, fingerprint-version. Changing the model or fingerprint-version triggers a full rebuild.

pr:section.pr:p: Concurrent writes are serialized via a lockfile .clayers/search/.lock; parallel clayers search invocations cannot corrupt the index or meta store.

pr:section.pr:title: Incremental Rebuild
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How rebuilds reuse unchanged embeddings by keying on the spec's own C14N node hash.
pr:section.pr:p: Each chunk carries a node-hash computed via clayers-spec's compute_node_hash helper — the same path the artifact drift detector uses. On rebuild, the indexer joins the chunk stream with meta.sqlite.nodes. Matching hashes keep the existing usearch key; mismatches re-embed.

Nodes that vanished from the spec (no chunk for their stored id) are deleted from both meta and usearch. New nodes are inserted with fresh keys. The indexer logs re-embedded: N nodes per run for CI verification.
Model or fingerprint-version changes invalidate every stored embedding. In that case the indexer discards meta.sqlite and rebuilds from scratch.

pr:section.pr:title: Command-Line Surface
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Flags, defaults, and the post-filter semantics of `--xpath` and `--layer`.
pr:section.pr:synopsis: clayers search "QUERY" [PATH] [--alpha F] [--beta F] [--k N] [--xpath EXPR] [--layer L[,L...]] [--json]
pr:section.pr:p: Two entry points share the search subcommand: querying runs a ranked search, while clayers search index [PATH] builds or updates the index without querying.

pr:property.pr:propdesc: Weight on the cosine-over-text component. Default is 0.7.
Weight on the Tanimoto-over-structure component. Default is 0.3; --beta 0 yields pure semantic search.
Keep only hits whose id is in the set returned by the spec-level xpath_to_id_set helper.

pr:section.pr:p: Output is a ranked table of id, file:line, layer, score (total), text_score, struct_score, and a preview. --json emits machine-readable output for agent pipelines.

pr:section.pr:title: Multi-query mode
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: One CLI invocation, N parallel natural-language queries, results unioned by id with the max score kept per node.
pr:section.pr:p: Agents that paraphrase a user question to hedge vocabulary mismatch need to run the same search with two or three worded variants. Doing that as three separate CLI invocations pays three warm-up costs and returns three JSON envelopes the caller must merge. The multi-query mode folds those into a single call.
--also is a repeatable flag carrying one additional natural-language query string. Any number may be given; the positional QUERY becomes optional once at least one --also is supplied.

Semantics: the engine runs one semantic search per variant (positional + all --also entries), collects the top-2k candidates from each, then unions the results by id keeping the highest score seen across variants. The merged set is re-sorted by score descending and truncated to the requested --k. --xpath and --layer post-filters apply to every variant identically.

JSON output is the same shape as single-query mode (a list of hit records). Callers that want to know which variant produced the winning score can re-run a single query against a specific id; the merged output does not track provenance today.

pr:section.pr:title: Adopt Integration (.gitignore)
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How `clayers adopt` keeps the sidecar out of git idempotently.
pr:section.pr:p: The sidecar directory .clayers/search/ holds derived artifacts that must never enter source control. clayers adopt creates or extends .gitignore with a .clayers/search/ entry bounded by sentinel comments # clayers:adopt:start and # clayers:adopt:end, mirroring the existing marker pattern adopt uses in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md.

Re-running adopt replaces everything between the markers in place, never duplicating entries. Pre-existing user-authored lines outside the markers are preserved verbatim. The freshness check in clayers adopt --update detects a tampered or missing block and re-plants it.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding semantic search as a complement to XPath querying, and when to prefer each.

org:reference.org:purpose: End-to-end pipeline reference: chunker, fingerprint, embedder, index, metric, CLI.
Rationale and mechanics of the two-pass chunker: pre-assembly span scan joined with combined-document context.
Bit-level layout of the 256-bit structural fingerprint and the segment responsibilities.
File-by-file reference for the `.clayers/search/` sidecar: index.usearch, meta.sqlite, config.json.

org:concept.org:purpose: How the indexer reuses unchanged embeddings via node-hash matching against the persisted meta store.

org:task.org:purpose: Use the clayers search CLI to build indexes and run ranked queries over a spec directory.
Extend clayers adopt so the sidecar index directory is gitignored idempotently.

rel:relation.rel:note: Semantic search complements XPath query by accepting natural-language input and ranking nodes by meaning.
The pipeline assembles the combined document to collect per-node context for chunking.
Chunker behaviour is a refinement of the end-to-end pipeline overview.
The search cache reuses the same C14N node-hash path maintained by the artifact drift detector.
The fingerprint layout is one of the two parallel pipelines described in the overview.
Sidecar layout records the outputs the pipeline produces.
The CLI is the user-facing surface for the pipeline.
Adopt gains a new responsibility: managing the .gitignore entry for the search sidecar.
The custom metric interprets the two halves of the concatenated fingerprint differently.
Incremental rebuild keys on state persisted in the sidecar's meta store.
Tanimoto is the similarity measure used on the 256-bit structural half.
Context-prepended chunks are produced by the two-pass chunker.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Jina-v2
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Larger context window but also larger model (~140 MB) and higher latency. Over-specified for spec-sized corpora (hundreds of nodes, not millions).

dec:alternative.dec:title: E5-base
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Stronger multilingual coverage but English benchmarks favour BGE-small. Multilingual specs are not a v1 requirement.

dec:alternative.dec:title: candle-transformers with a hand-picked model
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Pure-Rust without ONNX, but demands more boilerplate and locks us to whatever models have been ported to candle.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Stock cosine + Rust-side rerank of top-N
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Works without custom metric support but biases HNSW traversal toward the text half, hurting recall for structurally-similar, textually-different nodes.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Two parallel indexes + reciprocal-rank fusion
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Doubles storage, doubles build time, forces the CLI to run two searches and merge results.

dec:alternative.dec:title: User-cache location (~/.cache/clayers/search/<spec-hash>/)
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Hides the index from the spec directory, but decouples it from the spec's own lifecycle (git clean, branch switch). Loses locality.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Workspace-global index
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: One index per machine instead of one per spec. Cross-spec leakage and no per-spec model choice.

dec:alternative.dec:title: 128-bit compact
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Halves storage but collisions in the 128-bit ancestor-path segment rise to ~12% on specs with deep hierarchies. Recall degrades noticeably.

dec:alternative.dec:title: 1024-bit high-fidelity
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Near-zero collisions but 4x storage. No observed quality gain on spec scale (hundreds of nodes).

dec:alternative.dec:title: Pre-filter via usearch filter API
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Evaluates the id predicate during graph traversal; in principle cheaper for very selective filters, but biases the HNSW entry points and surfaces fewer true nearest neighbours.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Sequential scan
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Skip usearch entirely when --xpath narrows to a small set. Adds a second code path for a rare case.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Force-unset HF_HOME at embedder init
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Guarantees our with_cache_dir wins, but overrides the user's explicit environment choice.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Fail fast if HF_HOME is set
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Surfaces the precedence clearly but irritates users on managed environments where HF_HOME is set by policy.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Pin to fastembed 4.x
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Uses the InitOptions API the original plan prose assumed. But InitOptions is now #[deprecated] and breaking changes in 5.x cannot be cherry-picked.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Unpinned (^latest)
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Picks up upstream bug fixes automatically but exposes the workspace to surprise breakage.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Off by default; opt-in via --features semantic-search
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Keeps the base binary minimal (no ONNX, no tokenizer, smaller `cargo install` footprint) but adds friction for every user who wants search. Most users do want search; this is a high-value feature that shouldn't require a flag.

dec:alternative.dec:title: Always on (no feature flag at all)
dec:alternative.dec:rationale: Simplest code path but leaves no escape hatch for minimal-binary CI jobs or embedded users for whom the prebuilt ONNX runtime download is unwelcome.

pln:plan.pln:title: Implement `clayers search`
pln:plan.pln:overview: Ships the clayers search semantic-search subcommand across a new crate (clayers-search), two API additions in clayers-spec and clayers-xml, CLI wiring in clayers, and adopt/.gitignore integration. Execution mirrors the seven code steps in the ~/.claude/plans/ plan file and is verified by the workspace pytest checklist.

pln:item.pln:title: Scaffold clayers-search crate and feature flag
pln:item.pln:description: New workspace crate with fastembed, usearch, rusqlite deps. New semantic-search Cargo feature in the clayers crate, bundled ON by default with an opt-out path via --no-default-features.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: crate exists, feature declared, existing commands build with --no-default-features. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k test_step_01 -v

pln:item.pln:title: Chunker + compute_node_hash + xpath_to_id_set
pln:item.pln:description: Two-pass chunker in clayers-spec. Public compute_node_hash wrapper in clayers-spec. Public xpath_to_id_set in clayers-xml plus spec-level wrapper.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: Chunker emits records with id/file/lines/layer. compute_node_hash and xpath_to_id_set have unit tests that run and pass. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k "test_step_02 or test_step_02b or test_step_02c" -v

pln:item.pln:title: Structural fingerprint encoder
pln:item.pln:description: 256-bit fingerprint encoder with the six segments documented in search-structural-fingerprint. Deterministic across runs; snapshot-tested.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: Rust fingerprint unit tests pass; identical chunks produce identical bits. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k test_step_03 -v

pln:item.pln:title: Embedder, index, meta store
pln:item.pln:description: fastembed 5 TextEmbedding wrapper, usearch index with custom metric via change_metric, SQLite meta store. Incremental rebuild via node-hash.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: search index PATH creates the three sidecar files and config.json records the expected dims. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k test_step_04 -v

pln:item.pln:title: CLI wiring
pln:item.pln:description: clayers search subcommand with all flags (alpha, beta, k, xpath, layer, model, rebuild, json, text, verbose). Post-filter on top-2k candidates.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: search returns ranked hits, --xpath narrows to the requested layer, --beta 0 zeros the struct component. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k test_step_05 -v

pln:item.pln:title: Adopt .gitignore integration
pln:item.pln:description: gitignore.rs module; plant/check/update path via sentinel markers; idempotent; preserves user lines.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: adopt writes .clayers/search/ inside sentinel markers, idempotent on repeat runs, preserves user content. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k test_step_06 -v

pln:item.pln:title: Hash reconciliation, coverage, docs, release
pln:item.pln:description: Replace placeholder hashes, fill real line ranges, verify coverage + connectivity, flip plan status to done, extend bump-version.py + crates.yml, document in README and AGENTS.md.

pln:acceptance.pln:criterion: Drift is clean, coverage lists no uncovered search-* nodes, connectivity shows no isolated search-* nodes, plan status is done, release plumbing includes the new crate. Verify with: uv run --script $INFERAL_WORKSPACE/.inferal/checklists/right-now-we-re-limited-gentle-cat.py -k test_step_07 -v

art:mapping.art:note: Two-pass chunker: per-file span scan + combined-doc walk. Step 7 reconciles placeholder hashes and line range.
pub fn compute_node_hash wrapper exposing the previously private find_node_by_id + C14N path used by artifact --drift. Step 7 reconciles.
pub fn xpath_to_id_set in clayers-xml powering the --xpath post-filter. Step 7 reconciles.
256-bit structural fingerprint encoder with the six segments documented in search-structural-fingerprint. Step 7 reconciles.
fastembed 5 TextEmbedding wrapper. Honours HF_HOME, logs effective cache path. Step 7 reconciles.
Incrementally builds the usearch index with the custom metric via change_metric. Reconciles meta.sqlite and manages the file lock. Step 7 reconciles.
SQLite schema, reads, writes, incremental-rebuild cache. Step 7 reconciles.
search subcommand wiring in the top-level CLI. Covers the Clap subcommand definition + dispatch.
Subcommand handler: parses flags, dispatches to run_query (single) or run_multi_query (see map-search-multi-query).
run_multi_query: runs clayers_search::query::run per variant, unions by id keeping max score, sorts descending, truncates to k. JSON output matches single-query shape.
plant_gitignore + check_gitignore_freshness, sentinel- bounded, idempotent. Step 7 reconciles.
Call-site for plant_gitignore + freshness check extension inside adopt::adopt. Step 7 reconciles.

spec:clayers.llm:node: Semantic search is the clayers search subcommand: accepts natural- language queries, ranks spec nodes by meaning, complements XPath query (which requires known ids). Structure participates via the structural fingerprint; --alpha/--beta tune the semantic/structural mix; --beta 0 runs pure-semantic search.

Pipeline: two-pass chunker produces one chunk per @id-bearing node with (id, file, line range, layer, namespace, text with [layer=... path=...] header, node hash). Text half embedded with fastembed (BGE-small-en-v1.5 384-dim). Structural half computed as a 256-bit fingerprint. Both written to .clayers/search/index.usearch as one concatenated 640-dim vector. meta.sqlite stores per-node metadata and doubles as incremental-rebuild cache. Queries embed user string, pad struct half zeros, search via usearch custom metric alpha*cos + beta*tanimoto.

Chunker reads each file twice. Pass 1: xot parse_with_span_info to get byte-offset spans per @id element; byte_to_line helper converts to 1-indexed line numbers. Pass 2: combined-document walk for ancestor chain, layer, namespace, and relation incidence. Joins on @id to produce final chunks.

256-bit vector: bits 0-10 layer one-hot, 11-42 namespace URI hash, 43-170 ancestor-path 3-gram shingles, 171-186 element-name hash, 187-218 outgoing relation types, 219-250 incoming relation types, 251-255 reserved. Layout frozen; changes require bumping fingerprint-version in config.json and rebuilding.

.clayers/search/ holds three files. index.usearch is a memory- mappable usearch index of 640-dim f32 vectors (384 text + 256 struct-bits-as-f32). meta.sqlite has table nodes(id, file, line_start, line_end, layer, namespace, node_hash, preview, key). config.json stores model, dims, build timestamp, spec revision, fingerprint-version. Lockfile .clayers/search/.lock serializes concurrent builds.

On rebuild, indexer joins the fresh chunk stream with meta.sqlite.nodes by id. Matching node_hash keeps the usearch key and skips embedding. Mismatches re-embed. Vanished ids are deleted. New ids get fresh keys. Model or fingerprint-version change drops meta.sqlite and rebuilds from scratch. Logs "re-embedded: N nodes" per run.

Two entry points under `clayers search`. `search "QUERY" [PATH]` runs a ranked query. `search index [PATH]` builds/updates the index without querying (useful in CI). Flags: --alpha/--beta (metric weights, defaults 0.7/0.3), --k (top-k, default 10), --xpath EXPR / --layer L[,L…] (post-filter on top-2k), --model, --rebuild, --json, --text, --verbose. Output: ranked id, file:line, layer, scores, preview.

clayers adopt writes/extends .gitignore with .clayers/search/ entry between sentinel markers # clayers:adopt:start and # clayers:adopt:end. Idempotent across adopt --update. Preserves user-authored lines outside markers. Freshness check in adopt detects missing/tampered markers and replants.
Natural-language retrieval mode over spec nodes. Embeds the query, compares via a custom metric that combines cosine over text and Tanimoto over structural fingerprints. Complements XPath query.
A 256-bit vector encoding a node's structural position: layer, namespace, ancestor-path n-grams, element name, relation types. Enables structural similarity scoring independent of text.
The stored per-node vector: 384-dim text embedding + 256-bit structural fingerprint (stored as 256 f32 0.0/1.0s), totalling 640 f32 dimensions. One index, two signals.
.clayers/search/ directory: index.usearch, meta.sqlite, config.json. Derived artifact, gitignored by clayers adopt, never in source control.
Re-embeds only nodes whose C14N content hash changed since the last build. Reuses the artifact drift detector's node-hash path via pub fn compute_node_hash in clayers-spec.

Rust closure registered with usearch via Index::change_metric. Signature: fn(*const f32, *const f32) -> f32. Invoked during HNSW traversal. Computes alpha*cosine(first 384 dims) + beta*tanimoto (last 256 dims as bits). Derisk H2 confirmed invocation via inverted-metric negative control.

The embed-string for one node: "[layer=<layer> path=<ancestors>]\n<node text>". Header injects structural context into the dense text embedding so the same phrase in different layers ranks differently.

|A & B| / |A | B| on binary vectors. Standard in cheminformatics fingerprint search. Applied here to the 256-bit structural half so nodes sharing layer + ancestors + relations score similar regardless of prose.
Chose BGE-small-en-v1.5 via fastembed 5.x. Alternatives Jina-v2 (too large), E5 (multilingual overkill), candle-transformers (more code, narrower model coverage). Validated in derisk H1.

Chose usearch Index::change_metric with a Rust closure. Alternatives: stock cosine + post-rerank (biases HNSW, loses structural recall), two-index + reciprocal-rank fusion (2x storage, 2x build). Custom metric confirmed in derisk H2 via inverted-metric negative control.
Chose .clayers/search/ colocated with the spec. Alternatives: ~/.cache/clayers/... (decouples from spec lifecycle, loses locality), workspace-global (cross-spec leakage). Gitignored by clayers adopt.

Chose 256 bits with six segments. Alternatives: 128-bit (too collision-prone on deep hierarchies), 1024-bit (4x storage, no quality gain on spec scale). Layout frozen; bumps require fingerprint-version change + full rebuild.

Chose post-filter on top-2k candidates from usearch. Alternatives: usearch filter API (biases HNSW entry points), sequential scan (adds second code path). Post-filter preserves recall with one code path.

Chose to respect user's HF_HOME and log the effective cache path. Alternatives: force-unset (overrides user intent), fail-fast (irritates managed-environment users). Derisk H8 confirmed hf-hub honours HF_HOME first.
Chose fastembed = "5" (validated at 5.13.2 in derisk H1). Alternatives: pin to 4.x (deprecated InitOptions), unpinned (surprise breakage risk).

Chose to bundle semantic-search in the default feature set of the clayers crate. Most users want search; making it opt-in adds friction. Users who need a minimal binary (no ONNX, small footprint) opt out with --no-default-features at build time.
spec:clayers.art:exempt: Implementation plan; its items map to spec sections and code via the art:mapping entries above, not directly.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist, not by a specific code location.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist.
Plan item; acceptance witnessed by the pytest checklist.
Decision record; implementing code is covered by map-search-embedder.
Decision record; implementing code is covered by map-search-indexer.
Decision record; implementing code is covered by map-search-fingerprint.
Decision record; implementing code is covered by map-search-subcmd and map-search-xpath-id-set.
Decision record; implementing code is covered by map-search-embedder.
Decision record; implementing code is covered by map-search-embedder.
Decision record about Cargo feature defaults; implementing config lives in crates/clayers/Cargo.toml and is not a typical art:range target.
Vocabulary term; referenced by prose but has no direct code artifact.
Vocabulary term; implementing code covered by map-search-fingerprint.
Vocabulary term; implementing code covered by map-search-indexer.
Vocabulary term; implementing code covered by map-search-indexer.
Vocabulary term; implementing code covered by map-search-chunker.
Vocabulary term; implementing code covered by map-search-indexer.
